title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment and install all dependencies needed for CESF implementation, dataset handling, and evaluation

steps.1.1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.1.2: Install core dependencies: pip install numpy pandas scikit-learn scipy pyyaml jsonschema
steps.1.3: Install data handling: pip install openpyxl xlrd requests
steps.1.4: Install evaluation dependencies: pip install editdistance jellyfish
steps.1.5: Verify installation: python -c 'import pandas, numpy, sklearn; print("OK")'
steps.1.6: Create directory structure: mkdir -p data/{raw,clean,corrupted} results/{coverage,baselines,ablations} src tests logs

title: Download and Prepare Standard Datasets
description: Acquire Hospital, Flights, Food, and Adult datasets with known ground truth for validation and benchmarking

steps.2.1: Download Hospital dataset: wget -O data/raw/hospital.zip https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~woden/assets/hospital-dirty.zip && unzip -o data/raw/hospital.zip -d data/raw/hospital/
steps.2.2: Download Flights dataset: wget -O data/raw/flights.zip https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~woden/assets/flights-dirty.zip && unzip -o data/raw/flights.zip -d data/raw/flights/
steps.2.3: Download Food dataset: wget -O data/raw/food.zip https://www.cs.cmu.edu/~woden/assets/food-dirty.zip && unzip -o data/raw/food.zip -d data/raw/food/
steps.2.4: Download Adult dataset: wget -O data/raw/adult.data https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/adult/adult.data && wget -O data/raw/adult.names https://archive.ics.uci.edu/ml/machine-learning-databases/adult/adult.names
steps.2.5: Extract clean versions by removing errors using ground truth annotations
steps.2.6: Standardize schema: convert to CSV with consistent column naming, handle missing values uniformly
steps.2.7: Compute dataset statistics: rows, columns, domain sizes per column, FDs/constraints

title: Analyze Real-World Error Distributions for Taxonomy Validation
description: Analyze dirty datasets to validate that 8 error types cover real-world error distributions (RQ4 validation)

steps.3.1: Load dirty and clean versions of Hospital, Flights, Food, Adult datasets
steps.3.2: Identify error cells by comparing dirty vs clean versions (ground truth)
steps.3.3: Classify each error into taxonomy categories using pattern matching and heuristics:
steps.3.3a: Syntactic errors: typos (keyboard distance), formatting (case/punctuation), whitespace
steps.3.3b: Structural errors: FD violations (functional dependency checks), DC violations (denial constraints), key violations
steps.3.3c: Semantic errors: outliers (statistical z-score > 3), implausible values (domain violations)
steps.3.4: For unclassified errors, categorize as 'other' and record characteristics
steps.3.5: Compute coverage statistics per dataset and overall average
steps.3.6: Generate validation report: results/taxonomy_validation_report.json with coverage percentages
steps.3.7: Success criterion: Average coverage ≥85% across all datasets

title: Implement CESF Core Components
description: Build the CESF framework with error taxonomy, synthesis engine, and coverage analyzer

steps.4.1: Implement src/error_taxonomy.py with 8 error classes: TypoError, FormattingError, WhitespaceError, FDViolationError, DCViolationError, KeyViolationError, OutlierError, ImplausibleValueError
steps.4.2: Implement src/deterministic_rng.py: seeded random number generator for reproducibility
steps.4.4: Implement error generation functions for each type with context awareness
steps.4.5a: type_coverage(): Fraction of error types present in generated benchmark
steps.4.5c: detectability_score(): Fraction detectable by given constraints
steps.4.5d: repair_difficulty_index(): Log of candidate repair space size
steps.4.6: Implement src/config_parser.py for YAML/JSON configuration loading
steps.4.7: Implement src/main.py as CLI entry point
steps.4.8: Write unit tests for each component in tests/

title: Implement BART Baseline Error Generator
description: Reimplement BART's core error generation for fair comparison (rule-based, constraint violations, random mutations)

steps.5.1: Implement src/baselines/bart_generator.py based on BART paper [2]
steps.5.2: Implement denial constraint violation generation (BART's primary error type)
steps.5.3: Implement random character mutations: swaps, deletions, insertions
steps.5.4: Implement controlled detectability mechanism (greedy error placement)
steps.5.5: Ensure BART generates deterministic output with seed parameter
steps.5.6: Test BART on Hospital dataset: verify it produces constraint-violating errors

title: Implement Random Corruption Baseline
description: Simple random corruption baseline for comparison

steps.6.2: Generate purely random character-level mutations without structure
steps.6.3: No constraint awareness, no error taxonomy
steps.6.4: Test on sample data to verify basic functionality

title: Generate Benchmarks with CESF
description: Generate corrupted datasets using CESF with various configurations for comprehensive evaluation

steps.7.1: Create configuration files in configs/ for each dataset:
steps.7.1a: Standard config: balanced error distribution across all 8 types, 5% overall error rate
steps.7.1b: High-semantic config: emphasize semantic errors (outliers, implausible values)
steps.7.1c: High-syntactic config: emphasize typos and formatting errors
steps.7.1d: High-structural config: emphasize FD and constraint violations
steps.7.2: Generate benchmarks for Hospital dataset with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 999):
steps.7.2a: CESF output: data/corrupted/hospital_cesf_seed{N}.csv + ground truth JSON
steps.7.3: Generate benchmarks for Flights dataset with 3 seeds
steps.7.4: Generate benchmarks for Food dataset with 3 seeds
steps.7.5: Generate benchmarks for Adult dataset with 3 seeds
steps.7.6: For each generation, compute and save coverage metrics

title: Generate Benchmarks with Baselines
description: Generate equivalent benchmarks using BART and Random baselines for comparison

steps.8.1: Generate Hospital benchmarks with BART (3 seeds, 5% error rate):
steps.8.2: Generate Flights benchmarks with BART (3 seeds)
steps.8.3: Generate Food benchmarks with BART (3 seeds)
steps.8.4: Generate Hospital benchmarks with Random corruptor (3 seeds):
steps.8.5: Generate Flights and Food benchmarks with Random corruptor (3 seeds each)
steps.8.6: Compute coverage metrics for all baseline-generated benchmarks

title: Reproducibility Evaluation (RQ2)
description: Verify determinism and measure variance across repeated runs

steps.9.1: Run CESF 10 times with identical config (Hospital dataset, seed=42, same YAML)
steps.9.2: Compute MD5 hash of each output file, verify all hashes match exactly
steps.9.3: Run BART 10 times with identical config and seed
steps.9.4: Verify BART outputs are identical (expected: yes, BART is deterministic)
steps.9.5: Compute variance metrics: should be 0 for deterministic generators
steps.9.7: Success criterion: CESF variance = 0 (all 10 runs produce identical output)

title: Coverage Analysis Comparison (RQ1)
description: Compare coverage metrics between CESF, BART, and Random baselines

steps.10.1: Compute all 4 coverage metrics for each generated benchmark:
steps.10.1a: Type Coverage@k: fraction of 8 error types with ≥k examples
steps.10.1b: Distribution Balance: KL-divergence from uniform target
steps.10.1c: Detectability Score: fraction detectable by FD/DC constraints
steps.10.1d: Repair Difficulty Index: average log candidate space
steps.10.2: Aggregate results by generator (CESF, BART, Random)
steps.10.3: Compute mean and std across 3 seeds for each dataset
steps.10.5: Test statistical significance using paired t-test
steps.10.6: Success criterion: CESF achieves type coverage ≥0.9, better distribution balance than BART

title: Implement Simple Cleaning Algorithms for Evaluation
description: Implement or adapt existing cleaning algorithms to evaluate on generated benchmarks

steps.11.2: Implement src/cleaners/fd_based_repair.py: detect and repair FD violations using violation detection
steps.11.3: Implement src/cleaners/pattern_based_repair.py: regex-based formatting error detection
steps.11.4: Implement src/cleaners/hybrid_cleaner.py: ensemble of above methods
steps.11.5: Each cleaner takes (corrupted_data, ground_truth) and returns (detection_f1, repair_accuracy)
steps.11.6: Test cleaners on small sample to verify functionality

title: Discrimination Power Evaluation (RQ3)
description: Evaluate whether CESF benchmarks better discriminate between cleaning algorithms

steps.12.1: Run all 4 cleaning algorithms on CESF-generated Hospital benchmarks (3 seeds):
steps.12.1a: Metrics: Precision, Recall, F1 for detection; Repair Accuracy for correction
steps.12.2: Run all 4 cleaning algorithms on BART-generated Hospital benchmarks (3 seeds)
steps.12.3: Run all 4 cleaning algorithms on Random-generated Hospital benchmarks (3 seeds)
steps.12.4: Compute spread (std dev) of F1 scores across algorithms for each generator
steps.12.5: Compute discrimination index: variance in performance rankings
steps.12.7: Success criterion: CESF shows >20% larger spread in algorithm F1 scores vs BART

title: Ablation: Coverage-Aware Allocation
description: Measure impact of coverage-aware error allocation vs random allocation

steps.13.1: Run CESF with full coverage-aware allocation (baseline)
steps.13.2: Run CESF with random error allocation (no coverage requirements)
steps.13.3: Compare type coverage and distribution balance between two variants
steps.13.4: Measure impact on algorithm discrimination (spread in F1 scores)

title: Ablation: Error Type Categories
description: Evaluate impact of removing each error category (syntactic, structural, semantic)

steps.14.1: Generate benchmarks without syntactic errors (only structural + semantic)
steps.14.2: Generate benchmarks without structural errors (only syntactic + semantic)
steps.14.3: Generate benchmarks without semantic errors (only syntactic + structural)
steps.14.4: Evaluate all 4 cleaning algorithms on each ablated benchmark
steps.14.5: Compare performance drops to identify which error types are most discriminating

title: Ablation: Detectability Verification
description: Measure impact of detectability verification on benchmark quality

steps.15.1: Generate benchmarks with detectability verification enabled (default)
steps.15.4: Measure impact on cleaning algorithm performance correlation

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Perform rigorous statistical tests on all experimental results

steps.16.1: For coverage metrics: perform paired t-tests between CESF and baselines
steps.16.2: For algorithm performance: perform ANOVA to test for significant differences
steps.16.3: For reproducibility: verify zero variance with consistency checks
steps.16.4: Report p-values and effect sizes (Cohen's d) for all comparisons
steps.16.5: Generate results/statistical_tests.json with all test results
steps.16.6: Flag comparisons with p < 0.05 as statistically significant

title: Final Success Criteria Verification
description: Verify all primary and secondary success criteria are met

steps.17.1: Criterion 1 (Determinism): Verify CESF produces identical outputs with same seed - PASS if hash match = 100%
steps.17.2: Criterion 2 (Taxonomy Validation): Verify 8 error types cover ≥85% of real errors - PASS if coverage ≥85%
steps.17.3: Criterion 3 (Coverage Metrics): Verify all 4 metrics implemented and validated - PASS if all metrics computed
steps.17.4: Criterion 4 (Integration): Verify benchmarks generated for 3+ datasets - PASS if Hospital, Flights, Food done
steps.17.5: Criterion 5 (Discrimination): Verify >20% larger spread vs BART - PASS if ratio ≥1.2
steps.17.6: Criterion 6 (Reproducibility): Verify consistent rankings across runs - PASS if variance = 0
steps.17.7: Generate results/success_criteria_report.json with pass/fail for each

title: Generate Figures for Paper
description: Create publication-quality figures showing key results

steps.18.1: Generate Figure 1: Error taxonomy visualization with 8 types and 3 dimensions
steps.18.2: Generate Figure 2: Coverage comparison bar chart (CESF vs BART vs Random)
steps.18.3: Generate Figure 3: Type coverage heatmap across datasets and error types
steps.18.4: Generate Figure 4: Algorithm performance comparison (F1 scores) with error bars
steps.18.5: Generate Figure 5: Discrimination power - spread in algorithm performance per generator
steps.18.6: Generate Figure 6: Ablation study results showing component importance
steps.18.7: Generate Figure 7: Taxonomy validation pie chart showing real-world error coverage
steps.18.8: Save all figures to results/figures/ in PDF and PNG formats

title: Generate Tables for Paper
description: Create LaTeX-formatted tables for paper inclusion

steps.19.1: Table 1: Dataset statistics (rows, columns, domain sizes)
steps.19.2: Table 2: Taxonomy validation results (coverage per dataset)
steps.19.3: Table 3: Coverage metrics comparison (CESF vs BART vs Random)
steps.19.4: Table 4: Cleaning algorithm performance on different generators
steps.19.7: Save tables to results/tables/ in .tex and .csv formats

title: Generate Final Summary Report
description: Compile comprehensive experiment summary with all key findings

steps.20.1: Aggregate all results from previous experiments
steps.20.3b: Detailed results for each research question
steps.20.4: Log total runtime and resource usage
